samples.xml_description: Implement an asynchronous FIFO, FIFO bit width and depth can be configured(parameter DEPTH = 16, parameter WIDTH = 8). Write clock signal used for synchronous write operations. Read clock signal used for synchronous read operations. Write reset signal. Defined as 0 for reset and 1 for reset signal inactive. Read reset signal. Defined as 0 for reset and 1 for reset signal inactive. Write increment signal. Used to trigger write operations. Read increment signal. Used to trigger read operations. Write data input. The width [WIDTH-1:0] is configurable and represents the data to be written into the FIFO. Write full signal. Indicates if the FIFO is full and cannot accept further write operations. Read empty signal. Indicates if the FIFO is empty and cannot provide any data for read operations. Read data output. The width [WIDTH-1:0] is configurable and represents the data read from the FIFO. FIFO bit width configuration. FIFO depth configuration. // Define the Gray code conversion function function [3:0] bin_to_gray; input [3:0] bin; begin bin_to_gray[3] = bin[3]; bin_to_gray[2] = bin[3] ^ bin[2]; bin_to_gray[1] = bin[2] ^ bin[1]; bin_to_gray[0] = bin[1] ^ bin[0]; end endfunction // Declare registers for storing the write and read addresses reg [3:0] waddr_bin; reg [3:0] raddr_bin; // Declare buffers for storing the synchronized write and read addresses reg [3:0] wptr_buff; reg [3:0] rptr_buff; // Declare registers for storing the write and read pointers reg [3:0] wptr; reg [3:0] rptr; // Declare registers for storing the synchronized write and read pointers reg [3:0] wptr_syn; reg [3:0] rptr_syn; // Assign the write and read pointers to their respective buffers assign wptr_buff = wptr; assign rptr_buff = rptr; // Assign the synchronized write and read pointers to the corresponding buffers assign wptr_syn = wptr_buff; assign rptr_syn = rptr_buff; // Assign the read data output to the read data input of the dual-port RAM module assign rdata = rdata_ram; // Assign the write full signal to indicate whether the FIFO is full assign wfull = (wptr == {~rptr_syn[3], rptr_syn[2:0]}); // Assign the read empty signal to indicate whether the FIFO is empty assign rempty = (rptr == wptr_syn); // Update the write and read addresses based on the write and read increment signals always @(posedge wclk or negedge wrstn) begin if (!wrstn) begin waddr_bin <= 4'd0; end else if (winc) begin waddr_bin <= waddr_bin + 1; end end always @(posedge rclk or negedge rrstn) begin if (!rrstn) begin raddr_bin <= 4'd0; end else if (rinc) begin raddr_bin <= raddr_bin + 1; end end // Convert the write and read addresses to Gray code always @(posedge wclk or negedge wrstn) begin if (!wrstn) begin wptr <= 4'd0; end else begin wptr <= bin_to_gray(waddr_bin); end end always @(posedge rclk or negedge rrstn) begin if (!rrstn) begin rptr <= 4'd0; end else begin rptr <= bin_to_gray(raddr_bin); end end Use dual-port RAM to implement the FIFO. Converts a binary value to Gray code. Binary value to convert. Converted Gray code. gray[3] = bin[3]; gray[2] = bin[3] ^ bin[2]; gray[1] = bin[2] ^ bin[1]; gray[0] = bin[1] ^ bin[0]; Increments a pointer value. Pointer value to increment. Next pointer value after incrementing. next_pointer = pointer + 1; Compares two pointers to check if they are equal. First pointer value. Second pointer value. Flag indicating if the pointers are equal. equal = (pointer_a == pointer_b); Updates the write address based on the write increment signal. Current write address. Write increment signal. Updated write address. if (write_increment_signal) begin updated_write_address = write_address + 1; end else begin updated_write_address = write_address; end Updates the read address based on the read increment signal. Current read address. Read increment signal. Updated read address. if (read_increment_signal) begin updated_read_address = read_address + 1; end else begin updated_read_address = read_address; end Checks if the FIFO is full based on the write and read pointers. Current write pointer. Current read pointer. Flag indicating if the FIFO is full. is_full = (write_pointer == {~read_pointer[3], read_pointer[2:0]}); Checks if the FIFO is empty based on the write and read pointers. Current write pointer. Current read pointer. Flag indicating if the FIFO is empty. is_empty = (read_pointer == write_pointer); Generates output signals based on the FIFO status. Current write pointer. Current read pointer. Flag indicating if the FIFO is full. Flag indicating if the FIFO is empty. Write full signal. Read empty signal. write_full_signal = is_full; read_empty_signal = is_empty; Updates the write pointer synchronizer based on the write clock. Current write pointer. Write clock signal. Updated write pointer synchronizer. if (write_clock) begin updated_write_pointer_synchronizer = write_pointer; end else begin updated_write_pointer_synchronizer = write_pointer_synchronizer; end end Updates the read pointer synchronizer based on the read clock. Current read pointer. Read clock signal. Updated read pointer synchronizer. if (read_clock) begin updated_read_pointer_synchronizer = read_pointer; end else begin updated_read_pointer_synchronizer = read_pointer_synchronizer; end end Initializes all registers to zero. write_address <= 4'd0; read_address <= 4'd0; write_pointer <= 4'd0; read_pointer <= 4'd0; write_pointer_synchronizer <= 4'd0; read_pointer_synchronizer <= 4'd0; write_full_signal <= 1'b0; read_empty_signal <= 1'b0; // Instantiate dual-port RAM module dual_port_RAM #(DEPTH, WIDTH) ram_inst ( .wclk(wclk), .wen(wen), .waddr(waddr), .wdata(wdata), .rclk(rclk), .ren(ren), .raddr(raddr), .rdata(rdata_ram) ); endmodule
